Author: Rob Manson
Title: Bring on 2026!
Text:
Bring on 2026!
Software is ephemeral now…
I created this WebAI toy with Gemini and a few smart prompts to get the design and UX right.
- It uses WebAI for the hand tracking
- Three.js for the effects
Try it here TrustIndex.today/sparkler/
Or use the code yourself — it’s Open Source on github https://github.com/robman/virtual-sparkler
Software is ephemeral now
This is a fun way to bring in the new year, but it’s also a good example of a broader transformation that’s happening.
More and more disposable or ephemeral software is being created using agentic coding and the web is the perfect way to deliver this.
But underneath this transformation is a bigger question.
If everything’s changing faster & faster,
what can you rely on?
On January 1st, 2026 I’ll be launching my new project that attempts to answer this question and track the impacts — check it out at the TrustIndex.today.
You can also read a recent post I wrote about how Software Development is changing https://medium.com/ai-advances/asking-will-ai-drive-growth-in-developer-roles-is-the-wrong-question-e41ab4bda2ad
Subscribe on substack to stay up to date: https://latentgeometrylab.robman.fyi.
